fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: California Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to California - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity california, how to transfer pharmacist license to california, pharmacist compact california, california pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: California Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Title Protection Only (Business and Professions Code Section 2585)
board.mailing_address: 1625 N Market Blvd, Suite S200, Sacramento, CA 95834-1929

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in California.

steps.title: Register for a BreEZe Account
steps.description: Create a new user account on the BreEZe Online Services portal at breeze.ca.gov, providing personal details and confirming your email.

steps.title: Submit the Pharmacist Application
steps.description: Log into your BreEZe account, navigate to the California State Board of Pharmacy, and complete the online Pharmacist Application form for licensure by examination.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Electronically upload or mail official transcripts, intern hours affidavits, and any other required supporting documents as instructed within the BreEZe application or on the Board's website.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprinting and Background Check
steps.description: Register for Live Scan (for CA residents) or submit fingerprint cards (for out-of-state applicants) for a criminal background check, ensuring the results are sent to the Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pass Licensing Examinations
steps.description: Register for and pass both the North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination (NAPLEX) and the California Practice Standards and Jurisprudence Examination (CPJE).

steps.title: Await Application Processing
steps.description: Monitor your application status via BreEZe and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the Board of Pharmacy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a California pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does California have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in California. To work in California, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to California?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in California while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in California
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in California, new applicants must create an account and submit their application through the BreEZe Online Services portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: On the BreEZe homepage, click 'New Users: Register Here', create a user ID and password, provide personal information, and confirm your email address. Once registered, log in to access the application for the California State Board of Pharmacy.
